timeline.label: Repurchased and retired 8,066 shares (~$928 average) under expanded buyback authorization, shrinking float to 141,666 shares.
One solar project entered 30-year operations term; additional solar entered development term; renewable leases became majority of revenue.
Wind project entered construction term; first solar operations term commenced; FY2024 owner cash normalized to $2.13M.
Little Colorado water adjudication parties (except U.S.) entered Settlement Agreement subject to federal legislation.
Completed Delaware re-domestication; Aztec Minerals LLC investment first disclosed.
Renewable development lease income reached ~$2.0M, surpassing grazing as primary cash engine.
Executed mineral exploration framework with third party for oil, gas, and helium (royalty if produced).
Navajo County adopted Aztec Area Land Plan governing future development of corporation and company land.
Aztec Land and Cattle Company incorporated; legacy Arizona land position established.

industry.structure: Private Arizona landownership with concentrated regional scale (~239k surface acres); revenue from ground leases to renewable developers and ranchers rather than commodity production.
industry.share_shift: Renewable ground rent is displacing grazing as the cash engine; institutional capital targets long-dated solar and wind sites in the Southwest.
industry.trend: Hyperscaler and utility-scale renewable build-out increases demand for developable land and transmission-adjacent acreage; water adjudication and OTC listing keep the name off index screens.
